risk_summary: The indicator measuring reduced incidence of resource-based farmer-herder conflicts shows no improvement, remaining at 10% baseline through June 2024, despite the project's explicit Crisis Prevention and Conflict Mitigation component (Component 3, $100M). This suggests conflict dynamics are persisting and the project's conflict mitigation interventions are not yet achieving measurable impact.
evidence_quote: Reduced incidence of resource-based farmer-herder conflicts (Percentage) - Baseline Value: 10.00 Dec/2021, Actual (Current): 10.00 10-Jun-2024, Closing Period Value: 7.00 Dec/2027

risk_title: Slow Operationalization of Conflict Mediation Platforms
risk_summary: Only 9 out of 22 planned conflict mediation platforms have been established and made operational as of June 2024, indicating slow progress in building institutional mechanisms for conflict resolution in livestock-related disputes. This delays the project's ability to address farmer-herder tensions.
evidence_quote: Number of conflict mediation platforms established and operational (Number) - Baseline: 0.00 Dec/2021, Actual (Current): 9 10-Jun-2024, Closing Period: 22.00 Mar/2028

risk_title: Delayed Implementation of Livestock Service Centers in Conflict-Prone States
risk_summary: The establishment of 2 Livestock Service Centers (LSCs) in Kano and Bauchi States is marked as 'Off-track' with zero disbursements as of June 2024, despite $19.5M allocated. These states are critical for conflict mitigation given their farmer-herder tensions. Delays in establishing these centers undermine the project's ability to provide neutral ground for livestock services and conflict prevention.
evidence_quote: Establishment of 2 Livestock Service Centers (LSCs) in Kano and Bauchi States (Text) - Off-track, Allocated Amount: 0.00 10-Jun-2024, PBC allocation: 19,500,000.00

risk_title: Incomplete Mobility Agreements for Herder Movement
risk_summary: Zero percent of targeted states have developed and adopted agreements to facilitate herder mobility as of June 2024, with a target of 60% by project closing. These agreements are critical for reducing resource-based conflicts by clarifying herding rights and routes. Their absence leaves a key conflict prevention mechanism unaddressed.
evidence_quote: Share of targeted states having developed and adopted agreements to facilitate mobility (Percentage) - Baseline: 0.00 Dec/2021, Actual (Current): 0.00 10-Jun-2024, Closing Period: 60.00 Dec/2027

risk_title: Inadequate Stock Route Protection Against Encroachment
risk_summary: No stock routes have been protected from encroachment as of June 2024, despite a target of 2,000 kilometers by project closing. Encroachment on traditional grazing routes is a primary driver of farmer-herder conflicts in Nigeria. The absence of progress on this indicator suggests vulnerability to escalating land-use tensions.
evidence_quote: Length of stock routes protected from encroachment (Kilometers) - Baseline: 0.00 Dec/2021, Actual (Current): 0.00 10-Jun-2024, Closing Period: 2,000.00 Dec/2027
